items.question: Which statement best describes unsupervised learning?
options: Training with input-output pairs to minimize prediction error
Learning structure from unlabeled data without predefined target labels
Learning policies from reward signals in an environment
Training only on synthetic data generated by a GAN
items.answer: Learning structure from unlabeled data without predefined target labels
items.explanation: Unsupervised learning uses unlabeled X to discover structure; it does not require a label column y.

items.question: Which task is a classic unsupervised problem?
options: k-means clustering of user behavior features
Next-token prediction with cross-entropy on labels
items.answer: k-means clustering of user behavior features
items.explanation: Clustering groups unlabeled points; the other options rely on explicit labels or constructed surrogate targets.

items.question: Why standardize features before distance-based clustering?
options: To guarantee clusters match business segments
So features with larger numeric ranges do not dominate distance
Because clustering requires normally distributed labels
To convert unsupervised learning into supervised learning
items.answer: So features with larger numeric ranges do not dominate distance
items.explanation: Euclidean distance is scale-sensitive; preprocessing defines the geometry clustering optimizes.

items.question: How does self-supervised learning differ from classical unsupervised structure discovery?
options: Self-supervised builds surrogate labels from data; classical unsupervised does not
Classical unsupervised always requires human labels
They are identical paradigms with different names
items.answer: Self-supervised builds surrogate labels from data; classical unsupervised does not
items.explanation: Self-supervised creates pseudo-targets (e.g. masked tokens); clustering/PCA model structure in X without such targets.
